info.name: Meetzy API
info.description: Postman collection for Meetzy app — Auth Login, Profile Update, and Video Calling. Total: 6 requests.

item.name: 1. Auth — Login
item.description: Single endpoint handles all auth flows — new user, profile setup, and returning user. token_id is always required. All other fields are optional. Flutter navigates based on isProfileComplete in the response.

exec: console.log('isProfileComplete: true → Flutter goes to Home Screen');
console.log('isProfileComplete: false → Flutter shows Profile Setup Screen');

request.description:
One API for all 3 Flutter scenarios. token_id is always required. All other fields are optional — remove them when not needed.

──────────────────────────────────────────
USAGE GUIDE
──────────────────────────────────────────

Scenario A — Login Screen (new or returning):
Send only token_id. Remove all other fields.
→ New user:      isProfileComplete: false → show Profile Setup Screen
→ Returning user: isProfileComplete: true  → go to Home Screen

Scenario B — Profile Setup Screen:
Send token_id + display_name + gender + country_name + country_code.
→ Backend saves profile, isProfileComplete: true → go to Home Screen

──────────────────────────────────────────
FLUTTER NAVIGATION RULE
──────────────────────────────────────────
if (res.data.user.isProfileComplete == false) → Profile Setup Screen
if (res.data.user.isProfileComplete == true)  → Home Screen

──────────────────────────────────────────
EXPECTED RESPONSE
──────────────────────────────────────────
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "isNewUser": true,
  "message": "Login successful",
  "data": {
    "accessToken": "eyJhbG...",
    "refreshToken": "eyJhbG...",
    "expiresIn": 604800,
    "user": {
      "id": 1,
      "email": "nirav@gmail.com",
      "displayName": "John Doe",
      "photoUrl": "https://...",
      "gender": "male",
      "countryName": "India",
      "countryCode": "IN",
      "isVip": false,
      "isOnline": true,
      "isProfileComplete": false,
      "walletBalance": 0,
      "createdAt": "2026-06-01T00:00:00.000Z"
    }
  }
}
```

item.name: 2. Profile Update
item.description: Allows user to fetch and update their profile. Use GET first to load current data, then PATCH to send only the fields that changed.

request.description:
Returns the full profile of the currently logged-in user.

Note: DB columns are snake_case (e.g. display_name, photo_url) but the JSON API response uses camelCase (TypeScript property names).

Expected success response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "displayName": "Nirav V",
    "bio": "Software Developer | Video chat enthusiast",
    "photoUrl": "https://...",
    "countryId": 5,
    "countryName": "India",
    "countryCode": "IN",
    "gender": "male",
    "isVip": false,
    "isOnline": true,
    "isProfileComplete": true,
    "coins": 0,
    "urls": [
      "https://...",
      "https://..."
    ],
    "wallet": { "balance": 0, "currency": "coins" },
    "createdAt": "2026-06-01T00:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-06-01T00:00:00.000Z"
  }
}
```
Updates user profile fields. All fields are optional — only send what needs to change.

item.name: 3. Video Calling
item.description:
Video calling uses 1 HTTP route + 2 WebSocket namespaces.

HTTP: GET /users/:id — loads partner info on call screen overlay.

WebSocket /matchmaking — handles finding, ringing, accepting, and skipping matches.

WebSocket /call — exchanges WebRTC Offer/Answer signaling directly between two devices.

New Upgrades:
- Anti-Failure Audio: microphones launch on all devices including mobile.
- Accurate Call Durations: calculated in single Node.js context, no negative time logs.
- Double-Spending Safe Coins: balance locked during purchase/spend, logs in purchased_coins and used_coins.

request.description:
HTTP Route: GET /users/:id

Loads partner name, photo, country, gender on the video call screen overlay when a match is found.

Set the `partnerId` collection variable before running this request.

════ WEBSOCKET — NOT A REST API ════

This is a WebSocket namespace, not HTTP.
Do NOT click Send here. Follow the steps below.

──────────────────────────────────────────
HOW TO TEST IN POSTMAN:
──────────────────────────────────────────
1. Click New → WebSocket Request
2. Enter URL: ws://localhost:3000/matchmaking
3. Under Headers add:
   Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
4. Click Connect

──────────────────────────────────────────
EVENTS TO SEND: FLUTTER → BACKEND
──────────────────────────────────────────

► match:joinQueue
{
  "userId": "{{userId}}",
  "gender": "male",
  "country": "IN",
  "isVip": false
}
→ Adds user to Redis matchmaking queue
→ Matching engine starts searching

► match:leaveQueue
{
  "userId": "{{userId}}"
}
→ Removes user from queue

► match:nextMatch  ← SWIPE RIGHT TO LEFT
{
  "roomId": "{{roomId}}",
  "userId": "{{userId}}"
}
→ Closes current room
→ Other user gets call:partnerLeft event
→ THIS user re-joins queue automatically
→ New match:found sent when new partner found

► match:ping  (send every 30 seconds)
{
  "userId": "{{userId}}"
}
→ Keeps user alive in Redis queue

──────────────────────────────────────────
EVENTS RECEIVED: BACKEND → FLUTTER
──────────────────────────────────────────

◄ match:waiting
{
  "message": "Looking for match...",
  "queueSize": 5
}

◄ match:found
{
  "roomId": "uuid-room-id",
  "partner": {
    "userId": "uuid",
    "displayName": "Jane",
    "photoUrl": "http://...",
    "country": "IN",
    "gender": "female",
    "isVip": false,
    "age": 23
  }
}

◄ match:error
{
  "message": "Queue error, please retry"
}

════ WEBSOCKET — NOT A REST API ════

This is a WebSocket namespace, not HTTP.
Backend DOES NOT process video.
Video flows peer-to-peer between devices.
Backend only relays SDP and ICE signals.

──────────────────────────────────────────
HOW TO TEST IN POSTMAN:
──────────────────────────────────────────
1. Click New → WebSocket Request
2. Enter URL: ws://localhost:3000/call
3. Under Headers add:
   Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}
4. Click Connect

──────────────────────────────────────────
EVENTS TO SEND: FLUTTER → BACKEND
──────────────────────────────────────────

► call:sdpOffer  (User A sends after match:found)
{
  "roomId": "{{roomId}}",
  "sdp": {
    "type": "offer",
    "sdp": "v=0\r\no=- ..."
  }
}
→ Backend relays to User B only

► call:sdpAnswer  (User B sends after receiving offer)
{
  "roomId": "{{roomId}}",
  "sdp": {
    "type": "answer",
    "sdp": "v=0\r\no=- ..."
  }
}
→ Backend relays to User A only

► call:iceCandidate  (both users send multiple times)
{
  "roomId": "{{roomId}}",
  "candidate": {
    "candidate": "candidate:123...",
    "sdpMid": "0",
    "sdpMLineIndex": 0
  }
}
→ Backend relays to other user in room

► call:end  ← CUT CALL BUTTON PRESSED
{
  "roomId": "{{roomId}}"
}
→ Room deleted from Redis
→ BOTH users receive call:ended event
→ BOTH users navigate to HOME screen
→ Call duration saved to MySQL
→ Nobody auto re-queues

──────────────────────────────────────────
EVENTS RECEIVED: BACKEND → FLUTTER
──────────────────────────────────────────

◄ call:sdpOffer      ← relayed from partner
◄ call:sdpAnswer     ← relayed from partner
◄ call:iceCandidate  ← relayed from partner

◄ call:partnerLeft   ← partner swiped away
{
  "message": "Partner left",
  "roomId": "{{roomId}}"
}

◄ call:ended         ← someone pressed cut call
{
  "message": "Call ended",
  "roomId": "{{roomId}}",
  "duration": 125
}
→ Navigate BOTH users to home screen

──────────────────────────────────────────
FULL CALL FLOW SEQUENCE
──────────────────────────────────────────
1.  Both users → match:joinQueue
2.  Both receive ← match:found (same roomId)
3.  User A → call:sdpOffer
4.  User B ← call:sdpOffer (relayed)
5.  User B → call:sdpAnswer
6.  User A ← call:sdpAnswer (relayed)
7.  Both → call:iceCandidate (multiple times)
8.  Both ← call:iceCandidate (relayed)
9.  WebRTC peer connected — Video starts
10a. Swipe: User A → match:nextMatch
     User B ← call:partnerLeft
     User A finds new match automatically
10b. Cut Call: User A → call:end
     Both ← call:ended → navigate to home screen
